author: phase-4-coordinator
content: Resolved by implementing the Marsaglia-Tsang Ziggurat algorithm (256 layers) in ferray-random/src/distributions/ziggurat.rs. Both standard_normal_single (f64) and standard_normal_single_f32 (f32) now route through the Ziggurat path; Box-Muller is gone. Layer tables are built lazily via LazyLock with fixed-point iteration from R=3.654152885361009 and V=0.00492867323399, so unused programs pay nothing. 6 new tests cover table monotonicity, peak closure, mean/variance, skewness/kurtosis, tail reachability, and f32/f64 consistency. Microbenchmark on release profile shows ~4.83 ns/sample f64 (207 Msamples/s) and ~3.89 ns/sample f32 (257 Msamples/s), competitive with NumPy's own Ziggurat. normal/lognormal/standard_normal_parallel all inherit the speedup for free.
